rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DOROTHY EDA DATI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Neither a petition for final distribution nor a report of status with a petition for additional time to administer the estate has been filed yet. Counsel for the second successor executor informally advised this Examiner that $30,000 in unclaimed assets had recently (in July) been discovered, but no Supplemental I&A has been filed yet. It does not appear that an accounting has ever been filed in this estate. There was real property, and it was apparently sold by the first executor, who apparently used the estate for his personal gain and then died without an estate. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANZ VON UCKERMANN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/28/21. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The amended second and final account filed on 12/30/20 was never signed by the other co-executor, Patricia Costello, despite this issue having been raised repeatedly. [CRC 7.103(c) and Probate Code Sections 1020 and 1023.] It appears that a second amended second and final (?) account will need to be filed, as this one proposed distribution that included 675-9 Chenery Street, when a Notice of Proposed Action was filed on 8/24/21, indicating the co-executors intended to sell that property to Louis Cornejo. In any event, this amended second account was for the period ending 7/31/20, which was already over a year ago. As with the first accounting, not all receipts and disbursements are adequately described. The detailed schedules supporting the summary of account must show the nature or purpose of each receipt or disbursement, the source of the receipt or name of the payee, and the date thereof. [Prob. C. Sect. 1062.] A description of what was filed with the Court or sent by UPS or for which property/ies insurance was paid, etc., must be part of the description of disbursements rather than, for example, just "Court Filing Fee" or "SFPUC", etc. Why was tax (presumably property tax) paid twice each time for "York"? Copies of tax bills may need to be filed. Why was the SFPUC Water bill for York on 7/24/19 $2,646.93? A copy of the bill may need to be filed. Why were copies sent to Vanguard on 8/23/19 when the estate had no accounts at or any other transactions with Vanguard? Why is rental income so low? Copies of lease agreements may need to be filed. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 12/17/21. If a second amend second account cannot be filed in time to give notice of it for 12/28/21 after it is filed and scanned, Counsel should contact the Examiner to request more time. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/21/2021 to be heard with related conservatorship account. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE BRITT-MARIE BOMAN TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For An Order Confirming Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: continued to 12/15/21. still no proof of proper service on the persons whose interests are affected by the petition but are not current beneficiaries of the trust. service under CCP 415.20, commonly called "substituted service" requires service on someone else, followed by mailing the copies to the individual, and can only be used if the pleadings cannot "with reasonable diligence" be personally servce. mailed notice in and of itself is not sufficient. new dealine for filing proof of proper service so the petition can be set for hearing is noon on 12/8/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RUSSELL ROBERT KYLE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The petitioner, who is the sole residual devisee under the decedent's holographic will sought to be admitted to probate, is the only person having knowledge of the decedent's handwriting. The petitioner did not see the decedent write and sign the will, but alleges that she has seen his handwriting hundreds of times. 2) The petitioner requests that a bond be fixed at $20,000. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.
